feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Community Enhancements - January 2024
entry.content:
While you were probably (at least we hope) enjoying some well-deserved time off during the winter holidays, we have been busy and have just released the following enhancements to our SAP Community.

A New Sort Option Has Come to Town

There had previously been a sort option "Creation Date" in the Product and Technology boards.  This sort option has been added to Groups now.  So, when viewing discussion threads within Groups, users have the option to sort by "Creation Date."  While there has always been a sort option for "Most Recent," this does not equate to the original creation date of the thread.  The "Creation Date" option is now available within the Sort By drop-down list.

 

Yes!  There's a (Subscribe) Button for That

Have you ever absorbed by a blog or discussion content and wished you could be notified of any and all responses and comments at the push of a button?  Well, now you can.  A subscribe button now appears on the top-right of the respective content.  No need to fumble around in a drop-down list.  You want it, just hit the button and its yours.  All the notifications when comments/answers are posted to a blog/discussion are now yours!

 

Time and Time Again

Time for posted events is typically shown in the time zone determined in the user's profile.  However, what if the user is not logged in?  No, there is no need to panic.  With this enhancement, whether the user is logged in or not, the event time will be shown based on the user's browser time zone setting.  

 

Let's Not Float Away

It's always a good idea to know where one is at any given time.  It ensures we act responsibly and correctly.  No less so in the SAP Community.  This is why we have delivered a new floating indicator to let users know if they are on a page for which there are restrictions.  Initially, the floating indicator will be used in the Partner groups, including the SuccessFactors group.  It will also be rolled out to closed groups as well (groups that require a user to join).

entry.title: SAP the Date: 25th SAP Stammtisch Ostschweiz am 11.2.2025
entry.content:
Bereits zum 25. Mal treffen wir uns zum lokeren Afterwork Bier im Restaurant Brauwerk in Sankt Gallen. 

Es wird bestimmt wieder ein unterhaltsamer Abend mit interessanten Persönlichkeiten,

Bitte melde Dich zum Stammtisch vom 11.2.25 hier an.
Wirst Du dabei sein?

Melde Dich doch auch auf zu unserer LinkedIn Gruppe hier an.
Bist Du schon Mitglied?

entry.title: A warm welcome to our new SAP Champions in January 2025
entry.content:
Wishing a warm welcome to our latest additions to the SAP Champions family! Being accepted into one of our programs is seen as a top recognition, so the members should be very proud indeed. It’s great to add a new diverse mix of both expertise and location to say the least. We plan to expand this further.

About the program
The SAP Champions program was founded in 2019 and is one of two prestigious programs in SAP Community, the other being the SAP Mentors program. The SAP Champions program provides the framework to enable our top contributors across SAP Community & ecosystem to operate at their full potential, while also connecting community leaders to one another.

About community
Community is all about connecting people. Seeing people learn from one another, share their ideas and experiences, support one another, get involved in discussions and much more!

Who are SAP Champions?
SAP Champions are the role models and leaders making SAP Community a more inclusive and receptive environment where all individuals are welcome to seek help and exchange knowledge. They have tremendous community spirit that radiates across SAP Community as well as in their own local communities where they reside. They are passionate people who advocate for software adoption and all things SAP Community related.

Heartiest congratulations to our newest members!

Daniel Mateo, SpainMarco Buescher, Germany
Hilal Kurt, TurkeyNatallia Shtura, Poland
Khaled Elghali, GermanySandra Solis, Mexico
Lluís Salvador, SpainTsovinar Chugaszyan, Germany
Manuel Robalinho, BrazilWalid Ben Sabeur, France


What makes a great SAP Champion?
Being a top contributor across SAP Community & ecosystem for one! Read more.

Help us to welcome them!
Please join us in welcoming these amazing candidates by leaving a comment below!



@DanielMateo @Hilal_Kurt @Khaled_Elghali @Lluis @MRobalinho 
@marcobuescher @natallia_shtura @Sandra_Solis @Tsovinar @walid 
@StephanieMarley @christoph_christophersen @lauraschmitz @marco_v_santos

entry.title: Defining Business Architecture: A Guide for Estimating Transformation Project
entry.content:
Why don't Business Processes go to the ERP Transformation Party?

Because they heard that "Change" will be there, and they're still trying to figure out their outfit from the last ERP update!

Problem Statement:

You are embarking on transforming your business by replacing/upgrading your 20-year-old Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) platform. You are in the Explore phase and looking for a realistic scope and budget for implementation.

Your goal is to implement effective and efficient processes on a new platform. However, in practice, processes are often inadequately documented. Significant uncertainty arises without a clear understanding of the degree of changes required to redesign and implement processes. This uncertainty complicates the estimation of scope and budget. To reduce this uncertainty, a business architecture that provides a structured approach to understanding and estimating the transformation required at the process level must be created.

Why it’s essential to understand Business Processes

Every organization (business, government, not-for-profit) follows business processes to create value by completing tasks in a given sequence. Using processes, business users exchange information, money and materials to create value (by completing a given task). The success of the business is dependent on the efficiencies and effectiveness of these processes. They may complete all steps using paper, phone calls, and fax or use 100+ well-connected systems. These processes may not be documented at all, partially documented, or not updated in years.

Small businesses don’t formalize or document business processes, as an individual task processor's memory can hold ‘how things are done here.’ Their record-keeping systems are simpler, and transaction volume is low. They rely on an individual’s capability to get the job done. It doesn’t mean they don’t have to deal with complexities during transformation, but due to the smaller scale of their operation, they can ‘manage’ transitions in technology and market conditions.

However, in large organizations, processes span various business units, departments and IT applications. Data/process steps overlap between systems, often managed through swivel chairs (duplicate entries) and barely linked interfaces. Many process variances arise due to individual preferences as they slightly twist/bend/adapt the system to accommodate their way of working. Often, this results in adding extra business rules/logic to the application. Over the years, as people change jobs and processes evolve, this extra logic becomes ‘illogical/redundant/burden’ aka Technical Debt that knows how to get rid of it.

Typical reasons for not documenting business processes:

  1. There is no clear ownership for documenting end-to-end processes. Business analysts often work in silos of their expertise and do not cross departmental boundaries. System analysts often focus on 'system steps' and interfaces and ignore manual tasks.
  2. Business users go through the daily motions of process execution based on ‘how things are done here,’ and they have a hard time describing end-to-end processes.
  3. Users and analysts are so busy completing assigned tasks that they lack the time, energy, and motivation to stop and document the process.
  4. Documenting business processes seems enormous when you extrapolate 100s of processes across 10s of departments utilizing multiple systems (applications).

Defining business architecture iteratively early in the project will resolve the above issues. 

How do you define a business architecture for your transformation project?

Business architecture is defined with two separate artifacts

1. Business Capability Model (BCM) that describes the complete set of capabilities an organization requires to fulfill its mission. It’s typically structured hierarchically in three levels and describes WHAT an organization needs to deliver business value and succeed.

 

Figure 1 - SAP Reference Business Capability Model (BCM)

2. A Business Process Model (BPM) describes the business processes of an enterprise based on a standardized set of value-creating activities. It’s typically structured hierarchically in levels and describes HOW these value-creating activities will be performed

Figure 2 - SAP Reference Business Process Model (BPM)

Linkage between BCM and BPM:

  • The lower process levels will identify which set of capabilities a process needs to execute effectively and which processes a particular capability will serve.

You can use hierarchical elements of BCM and BPM for various purposes. A few advantages of building hierarchies are:

  • Group higher levels into ‘Strategic,’ ‘Core,’ and ‘Supporting,’ per the organization's strategic objectives.
  • Performing heat mapping for capability maturity, risks, investment requirements
  • Ease of scheduling workshops and assign capability/process owners and subject matter experts.
  • Estimate implementation and change management efforts at various levels of the hierarchy

Note: SAP Reference Business Architecture provides template/industry variants for BCM and BPM - SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub.

The Business Roles catalog and Business Data catalog are two other business architecture artifacts created in the explore phase. They can be used as input to create business activity flowcharts. This blog does not cover business capabilities (L3) linking to Solution capabilities.

Why do you need to create a business process hierarchy?

According to the SAP Activate methodology, the business scope is defined in the Explore phase of the project. You will need the following to estimate the transformation scope/effort.

  1. List of business processes that are generally in scope (to be refined in Fit-to-Standard [F2S] workshop)
  2.  Scope of change at a process level including a) effort to configure the system for to-be processes and b) change management efforts 

The above is achieved by capturing delta requirements during fit-to-standard workshops.

A business process hierarchy (BPH) can structure a list of business processes that are part of the project's overall scope (also called a business process catalog). This process is iterative and typically incomplete until all F2S workshops are complete.

How do you create a business process hierarchy?

You can utilize BCM or BPM to create BPH.

Option 1: Business Capability Model (BCM) based grouping:

  • Business Domain (L1), Business Area (L2), Business Capability (L3) + Business Activity (L4)
  • A functional view of the business [difficult to stitch together E2E process]
  • Assing business process (L4) to one prime business capability (L3). Typically, there is more than one capability required to perform a given process, so assigning a prime will make it easy to visualize the hierarchy
  • Easier to identify links between business capabilities and solution capabilities

Option 2: Business Process Model (BPM) based grouping:

  • Business Process (L1), Business Process Module (L2), Business Process Segment (L3) + Business Activity (L4)
  • Process-centric [difficult to tackle duplicate capabilities within an organization]
  • It is easier to accommodate process variances at higher levels in the hierarchy.
  • It is difficult to assign a single end-to-end process owner as processes span multiple functions.
  • The nested nature of BPM makes it challenging to visualize in a tabular list.

  Figure 3 - Business Process Hierarchy example for "Manage Purchase Order"

A Business Activity (L4) can be referenced in multiple higher-level processes/process variances. Business activities can be nested inside other business activities to break complex business activities with many steps into manageable activities.

Business Activity (Level 4):

  • A 'granular business process' creates value for internal or external stakeholders.
  • will have consistent naming conventions
  • further broken down into tasks (level 5) and steps (level 6)
  • will have a unique process flow that is defined using ‘BPMN’ diagram
    1. Steps to perform in a given sequence with decision points
    2. Application/s [input from Application catalog]
    3. Data utilized/analyzed/updated/produced [input from Business Data catalog]
    4. Roles/agents responsible for the activity [input from Business Role catalog]

Fit-to-standard workshops will evaluate business processes at this level. During these workshops, define 'to-be' Business Process, create flowcharts (in BPMN), and capture delta requirements (backlog). Use SAP Best Practices as a starting point (reference SAP Signavio Process Navigator). 

Figure 4 - Understanding links between BCM, BPM, Business Activity, Task and Steps

Tips:

  • Start with the SAP reference content and tailor it to your industry/organization needs.
  • Defining the first three levels before diving into listing business activities (L4)
  • Involve Business and IT user community

Caution:

  • You can start this exercise on a spreadsheet, but it’s not scalable and not great for collaboration.
  • Get the right tool to manage BCM (e.g. #SAP LeanIX), BPM and BPH (e.g. SAP Signavio)
  • Setup naming standards

You have now defined the Business Process Hierarchy and have the list of all required business activities (L4). You are ready to execute a fit-to-standard workshop to capture the backlog <blog - Conduct F2S>. You can summarize backlog at various hierarchy levels to scope the estimated project cost and change management efforts.

Conclusion:

Defining a business architecture is a crucial step in any transformation project. It provides a structured approach to understanding and improving the processes that drive your organization. By mapping out these processes, you can identify inefficiencies, streamline operations, and ultimately achieve your transformation goals. Remember, the success of your project depends on transforming business processes (they need to attend the party!). So, take the time to get it right; your efforts will pay off in the long run.

Reference:

  1. TOGAF Value Streams Guide
  2. SAP Signavio Process Explorer – Accessed Jan 20, 2025 SAP Signavio
  3. Process Mapping Basics: Guiding Your Organization Toward Effective Process Mapping - SAP Signavio - Accessed Jan 20, 2025
  4. The Top 5 Mistakes You Can Make When Naming Business Processes - Accessed Jan 20, 2025
  5. Book: Business Architecture: Collecting, Connecting, and Correcting the Dots, by Roger Burlton. Technics Publication

entry.title: An Introduction to Documentation Versions on the SAP Help Portal for Functional Localization
entry.content:
I’m pleased to share the latest information on accessing documentation on the SAP Help Portal for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. In this blog post, I’ll explain how you can choose the right documentation version for your technical requirements and discuss the recent updates on the SAP Help Portal for maintenance phases.

Title image for the topic of documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal

I’ll be focusing today on the country/region-specific documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition that is published through the Documentation Delivery with SAP Notes process, though there are useful tips here for everyone who browses version-specific content on the SAP Help Portal.

1. Choosing the Right Documentation Version for You

The SAP Help Portal is your number-one resource for information on SAP products. When navigating documentation, it’s important to verify that the version you’re browsing is correct for your solution. You can do this by checking the Version Selector drop-down menu, as shown in this example from the Local Versions page:

Drop-down version selection on the SAP Help Portal

Why do we recommend this version verification step? Well, you can access SAP Help Portal documentation by many different means, such as links from SAP Notes and What’s New topics, as well as from search engines. You may be directed to the highest version, which is not necessarily the same version implemented by your company. Different versions may have different scope functionality described in the documentation, so it’s best to always check this drop-down menu when accessing the SAP Help Portal.

2. New on the SAP Help Portal: Maintenance Information

You might have noticed that, when you access documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, there’s a new information banner that specifies the maintenance phase:

Maintenance message now available on the SAP Help Portal

This information banner differentiates between two maintenance phases, Mainstream Maintenance and Extended Maintenance. If you are an SAP customer or partner with a valid SAP S-User ID, you can refer to the SAP Maintenance Phases resource for further details on this topic.

For the most recent documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal, you are informed that “documentation is updated with the support package stacks”. However, there is an important exception to this rule for country/region-specific updates delivered for functional localization.

3. Always Up-to-Date: Documentation Delivery for Functional Localization

For country/region-specific development delivered for functional localization, such as legal changes, a new process provides documentation updates between support package stacks. As detailed in the recent blog post on Documentation Delivery on the SAP Help Portal with SAP Notes, you now have access to SAP Help Portal documentation that reflects the latest information, regardless of the planned support package release dates.  

For both the mainstream and extended maintenance phases of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we’re glad to provide you with up-to-date information on how SAP meets the local legal requirement for your solution. 

To find more information on country/region-specific topics, I recommend the Localization topic page on the SAP Community. There you can find the latest news, resources, and upcoming events for SAP’s localized solutions. Remember, too, that you can always select the Create a Question option on the Localization topic page to engage with experts and resolve any doubts you may have:

Create a Question for Localization on the SAP Community

And for those of you who use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, you can find more details on the SAP Help Portal and SAP Community in our dedicated blog series:

  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Engaging with the SAP Community

That’s it for today, though please let me know in the comments section if you have any questions. Your feedback is always appreciated.

Thank you and until next time,

Barnabas

entry.title: Capturing Delta Requirements: A Guide to Fit-to-Standard Workshops
entry.content:
You are about to transform your business by replacing or upgrading your 15- or 20-year-old Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) platform. With a 'Cloud First' approach, you have presented the project's key objectives to management and chosen the SAP Business Suite as your future ERP. Your sandbox environment is set up with the Enterprise Management Layer and SAP Best Practices. Now, you are ready for transformation. What's next?

Fit-to-Standard (F2S) Workshops

As per the SAP Activate methodology, a few tasks happened during the explore phase.

  1. Define business architecture <blog>
  2. Perform a fit-to-standard analysis to capture Delta requirements (backlog) using SAP Signavio Process Manager. 

Points to note for process design during Fit-to-standard workshops

  • Workshops are driven by business process owners who are transformation champions and complimented by strong IT SMEs
    • This is an opportunity for the IT team to understand what outcome the business wants to achieve
  • Consider all relevant applications and steps to complete a business process
    • Don’t just focus on SAP-relevant steps. Include other system steps/interfaces, and don’t forget to capture manual steps
  • Create a structure of ‘Business Process Hierarchy’ before you begin workshops <blog>
    • The hierarchy will help you schedule workshops and assign subject matter experts
    • Hierarchy will help you to summarize/aggregate the findings at various levels

Capturing Delta Requirements in Fit-to-Standard Workshops

Fit-to-standard workshops are structured around Business Capabilities (focused on business or functional areas) or End-to-End Business Processes (focused on processes). These workshops explore functionality, demonstrate how solutions and best practices meet business requirements, and enable business process experts to define ‘to-be’ processes.

Delta requirements are identified, discussed, and then added to the backlog. The backlog consists of documented requirements, improvements, or functionalities required to complete a process. These items are prioritized, iteratively reviewed, and refined.

Understanding Business Process

Once you have defined the Business Process Hierarchy, you have a list of business activities.

Anatomy of a Business Activity (modular business process):

  • A 'granular business process' creates value for internal or external stakeholders.
  • A business activity is a combination of the following:
    1. Applications: Which applications will be used?
    2. Data: What data will be utilized, analyzed, created or updated?
    3. Users: Who is going to execute it? (Role/agent/AI!)
  • will have consistent naming conventions.
  • will have a unique process flow that is defined using a ‘BPMN’ diagram.
  • will have a clear start and end
  • will have a unique sequence of tasks (level 5) to be performed. Each task is divided into steps (level 6)
  • Tasks (L5) will be performed by a role/agent. They will utilize application/s where data is used/analyzed and output/value is created. Policies may exist, and the organization may have business rules to complete these tasks.
  • Each task (L5) will be broken down into steps (L6). These steps are performed in a given application or used by an interface. Steps will include required business logic/rules.

 Figure 1 - Understanding Business Activity Components

Understanding delta requirement collection

SAP provides standard solution flow diagrams and best practice diagrams. During the fit-to-standard workshop, delta requirements will be collected at multiple levels. The following diagram shows the level at which the delta requirements are captured.

 

 

 

Figure 2 - Capturing Detla requirements (backlog)

Process Focused

1. Confirm whether a process is required at a process level.

  • Examples:
    • Create a purchase order for services > Required
    • Creating sales orders for services > Not required

If additional processes are required, then it's a scope discussion with the project team. Beware of scope creep and the temptation to add/remove adjacent processes.

2. What other process variants exist

  • Examples:
    • Create a purchase order for the project purchase
    • Create a purchase order for the stock purchase
    • Create purchase orders for services based on contract

Caution: This is a slippery slope for future enhancements. More variants will result in more business logic and business rules, especially when different business units require variances to execute the same processes.

Application focused

3. Business rules: (expression of policy)

  • Examples:
    • Purchasing Approvals above a certain threshold
    • Applying tax codes and calculation

Note: Challenge outdated policies or rules that are no longer relevant. SAP SMEs should focus on configurable rules.

4. Business logic (expected system behaviours):

  • Examples:
    • Status updates for an outline agreement
    • Budget control checks

Note: Avoid convoluted and 'one-off' scenarios. Look for the intended outcome. Avoid adding rules that create detours and unnecessary stops.

5. Enhancement/extension requirement

  • Examples:
    • A field on the PO is required to indicate ‘recycled material.’
    • Derive material handling requirements for dangerous goods

Note: If business rules and business logic can not be implemented by application configuration, then additional extensions and enhancements are required. Remember that Clean Core starts with simple processes and data structure!

Tip: Always ask clarifying questions while defining business logic/business rules to minimize enhancement/extension. Implementing legacy logic (or illogic)  without rationalizing will create technical debt.

6. Interface requirement

  • Example:
    • Send purchase orders to a specific vendor via an electronic interface

Data/Information focused

7. Data requirement

  • Example:
    • Enterprise Structure: Company codes, Plants, Sale Org
    • Master data: Material, Vendor, cost center etc.
    • Transaction data: Purchase orders, Purchase requisition
    • Data requirements can be captured here, e.g., Data quality, retention, and validation.

8. Reporting requirement

  • Example:
    • Report to see purchases from a vendor in a given time (for ATIP)

9. Output/Forms

  • Example:
    • PO printouts (including PDF) as per Canada’s procurement guideline

User role/Security focused

10. Automation:

  • Example:
    • Auto approval for PO/IR less than a certain amount
  • Tip: Processes designed with clear business rules, business logic and data structure are prime for automation 

11. Security/Access control

  • Examples:
    • The purchasing officer can not enter goods receipt or invoice receipt
    • Mask credit card numbers

Rules of engagement during Fit-to-Standard (F2S) exercise.

  • This exercise simplifies processes by minimizing variances, establishing clear business rules and logic, and documenting precise requirements to achieve desired outcomes. Avoid documenting requirements as ‘as-is’. The status quo is not your friend.
  • Convoluted and contradictory rules/logic will result in messy processes with unnecessary coding that must be maintained for a long time.
  • Simplify processes and data structures. This will positively impact user experience and reduce application management costs for years.
  • Note that specific enhancements and integrations are inevitable if they must meet legislative requirements or interface with other existing systems. Focus on re/designing these extensions in a compliant manner.
  • Once these requirements are reviewed, refined, and approved, SAP SMEs can work on developing process design documents and creating specs for configuration, enhancements, extension, and integration. Additional requirements, such as data conversion and non-functional requirements, can be captured at a higher level.
  • Use SAP Signavio Process Manager attributes to capture requirements and notes. Define requirement categorization, priorities, dependencies, etc. Capture dependencies
  • Don’t squander this is an opportunity to get close to the user; consider this as a ‘design thinking’ exercise.

Conclusion:

While consultants and the project team will eventually move on to other projects, the users will continue to execute these processes for the foreseeable future. It's crucial to design and implement the best processes, as users rely on you to enhance their efficiency and effectiveness. Your thoughtful design will have a lasting impact on their daily operations.

Users are counting on you to bring out their best!

entry.title: Layouts Básicos e Floorplans do SAP Fiori
entry.content:
O layout básico do Fiori é o layout dinâmico (dynamic layout). Ele é dividido em cabeçalho, página e rodapé. Essa divisão ajuda o usuário a focar no conteúdo da página.

Outro tipo de layout é o de colunas flexíveis. Com ele, é possível exibir diferentes floorplans em uma única página, o que proporciona uma navegação mais rápida e fluida em comparação ao layout dinâmico, permitindo uma divisão de até 10 colunas.

Os floorplans, sejam eles pré-definidos ou customizados, podem ser encaixados nos layouts mencionados acima.

Dentre os floorplans que melhor se encaixam nos layouts, destacam-se:

  • Overview Page: Um aplicativo que exibe todas as informações que o usuário precisa em uma página, com base em seu perfil.

  • List Report: Permite aos usuários trabalhar com um grande volume de dados, onde é possível realizar filtros de busca e ações nos itens exibidos. Geralmente, é utilizado como porta de entrada para a exibição detalhada de um item em uma Object Page.

  • Object Page: Utilizado para exibir e categorizar informações de um único objeto, como uma ordem de venda. As informações podem ser acessadas por âncoras (links) ou abas.

  • Wizard: Permite ao usuário completar uma tarefa longa em seções. Consiste em um passo a passo onde cada parte do formulário é revelada a cada passo completado. Ao final, há uma página de resumo que exibe todos os dados preenchidos pelo usuário, prontos para submissão.

  • Initial Page: Permite navegar para um determinado registro ou editá-lo. O ponto inicial é um campo único exibido.

  • Worklist: Exibe uma coleção de itens que o usuário precisa processar. Permite revisar os itens da lista e tomar ações, como completar uma tarefa ou delegar para outro usuário.

  • Analytical Page (ALP): Oferece uma maneira única de analisar dados passo a passo sob diferentes perspectivas. Serve para identificar dados interessantes, já que os gráficos são interativos e filtráveis entre si. Por fim, permite agir em um registro específico.

entry.title: Recurring deduction future timeslice records mass deletion
entry.content:
Requirement:

Delete the future time slice data or pay components from recurring deductions portlet, we can use the below import process so that the a particular time slice record or a particular pay component will be removed from employees recurring deductions data.

Below sample employee has multiple records in recurring deductions, we must remove all pay component deductions which start from Jan.2026 for all employees.

Possible Solution:

Mass deletion of the required time slice and removing all deductions for employees can be done with the import.

Export data of existing employees recurring deductions with “Import and export data” with Generic object as “Recurring deduction” with including dependencies “Yes”.

  • To remove the deduction, pay components from future dated records –
  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items” to remove the Jan.2026 pay component records for the appropriate employees.
  2. Insert “DELIMIT” operation for the deductions which are not required as shown in below picture.
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items”.

 

  • To remove the Jan.2026 time slice for all the deductions available for user-

Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” this file will have all the employee recurring deduction records with employee number and the effective date of the deduction.

  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” to remove the Jan.2026 time slice.
  2. Remove the timeslice records not required( as highlighted in below picture) or Insert “DELETE” operation for the deductions which are not required
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction”.

Below screenshot shows the export file of recurringdeduction.csv

Below picture shows the import file which has the user records after removing the not required time slice.

For the below sample employee, Jan.2026 time slice is now removed from the employee recurring deductions portlet after the successful import.

This is how to delete Recurring Deductions from employee profile. Hope this blog is helpful.

entry.title: 1º Meet Up Salvador de 2025
entry.content:
Se você trabalha com SAP – seja consultor, usuário ou entusiasta – este encontro é para você! Vamos fortalecer a comunidade SAP na Bahia com muita troca de experiências, aprendizado e, claro, novas amizades.

✨ Por que participar?\

Networking com profissionais da área\

Dicas e truques valiosos para o dia a dia\

Momentos de descontração e muito aprendizado

Traga seu entusiasmo e prepare-se para um encontro inesquecível. Juntos, faremos a diferença na comunidade SAP na Bahia! 🌐💡

@Marssel700

entry.title: SAP Estende Suporte para Versões Antigas Até 2033
entry.content:
A Nova Estratégia da SAP para a Business Suite 7

A SAP decidiu que vai encerrar o suporte para a antiga Business Suite 7 e está pressionando seus clientes a atualizarem seus sistemas. No entanto, agora a empresa oferece uma alternativa para estender o uso desses sistemas – uma medida que beneficia tanto os clientes quanto a própria SAP.

Empresas que aderirem ao programa Rise with SAP poderão continuar utilizando seus sistemas antigos até o final de 2033. Originalmente, o suporte seria encerrado em 2027, e a manutenção estendida, que exige um custo adicional, seria encerrada em 2030. Isso significa que os clientes têm mais tempo para se adaptar, já que a migração para uma nova solução pode levar anos.

A SAP confirmou os planos e destacou que essa opção visa facilitar a transição para o Rise with SAP, garantindo a continuidade dos negócios de seus clientes. Essa nova alternativa é chamada de SAP ERP, private edition, transition option, mas mais detalhes, como os custos, só serão divulgados ao longo do primeiro semestre deste ano.

Apesar da prorrogação, a SAP deixou claro que o suporte para a Business Suite 7 terminará definitivamente em 2027. Para quem ainda não começou a transição, essa prorrogação pode ser um alívio, já que mais de 60% dos clientes ainda utilizam o ECC.

Em resumo: pague um pouco mais, respire até 2033 e tenha tempo para planejar a migração para a nova geração de produtos SAP.

Fonte:

https://www.handelsblatt.com/technik/it-internet/s-4hana-das-ist-saps-neue-strategie-zur-business-suite-7/100101152.html?paymentupdate=undefined

entry.title: SAP Communities: Sharing Knowledge and Connecting Professionals
entry.content:
SAP values ​​and encourages the development of its global communities, recognizing that knowledge exchange, networking, and collaboration are essential to the collective success of the ecosystem. Through programs such as SAP Champions and SAP Mentors, as well as events organized by local communities, SAP reinforces its commitment to connecting people and fostering innovation.

Global Programs: SAP Champions and SAP Mentors

SAP Champions, launched in 2019, celebrates leaders in SAP communities, not only for their technical contributions but also for their ability to inspire and engage other members. These professionals are essential in creating an inclusive environment and disseminating knowledge.

SAP Mentors bring together prominent influencers from the SAP ecosystem, such as customers, partners, and consultants. These mentors are recognized leaders in their fields and actively contribute to improving SAP solutions, sharing strategic feedback with product teams and executives.

Local Events: Fostering Collaboration and Knowledge

SAP Inside Track (SIT)

SAP Inside Tracks (SITs) are events organized by local communities to share experiences, success stories, and trends in SAP solutions. Recent examples include:

  • SIT Fortaleza (2024): This event, which brought together professionals at the Luzeiros Fortaleza Hotel, featured lectures on topics such as SAP Activate and SAP S/4HANA and promoted networking in the Northeast region of Brazil.
  • SIT Porto (2024): The inaugural edition in Portugal highlighted topics of local and global relevance, strengthening the SAP community in Europe.

SAP CodeJam

SAP CodeJams are hands-on events aimed at developers who want to deepen their knowledge of SAP technologies. During these sessions, participants can learn hands-on, experiment with SAP APIs and tools, and interact with experts. These meetings are often held in cities with SAP professionals, such as São Paulo, Lisbon, Porto, and Munich.

SAP Stammtisch

SAP Stammtisch, which takes a more informal approach, is a regular gathering organized by local communities. In a relaxed atmosphere, participants discuss solutions and trends and share challenges. These events are popular for creating authentic connections and fostering the exchange of ideas between consultants, customers, and partners.

The Role of Consulting Firms as Sponsors

A striking feature of these events is the collaboration of SAP consulting firms that act as sponsors and knowledge catalysts. These firms play a key role, contributing to the following:

  • Success Stories: Presentation of relevant projects carried out in the region, highlighting successful implementations, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, and other innovative solutions.
  • Workshops and Lectures: Sharing insights on specific challenges and solutions applied to different industries.
  • Networking: Facilitating connections between participants and business opportunities.

Local companies, such as large multinational and regional consultancies, strongly support events such as SIT and CodeJam, ensuring knowledge sharing from their geographies.

In conclusion, Events such as SAP Inside Track, SAP CodeJam, and SAP Stammtisch strengthen local communities, promote innovation, and create continuous learning environments. The support of consultancy companies is essential for the success of these meetings, consolidating the relationship between SAP, its partners, and customers.

In addition, SAP Champions and SAP Mentors play a fundamental role as articulators and motivators of local communities. They are responsible for organizing, encouraging participation, and ensuring that the content of the events is relevant and of high-quality events.

Join the SAP community and be a SAP Champion!

entry.title: Bem Vindos ao Grupo de Salvador da Comunidade SAP!
entry.content:
Olá a todos, deixe-me apresentar! Me chamo Aline Leite e resido em Camaçari região metropolitana de Salvador. Sou consultora especializada em SAP PM, PP e QM. Trabalho com SAP desde 2007, com ampla experiência em projetos de implementação end to end tanto em S4Hana quanto em ECC. Também participei de diversos projetos mobile, contribuindo para a integração e eficiência dos sistemas.Sou casada, mãe de três filhos, e encontro grande prazer em caminhar e desfrutar da serenidade do mar, que sempre me chama e renova minhas energias.

Mais sobre mim:

https://community.sap.com/t5/user/viewprofilepage/user-id/1447468

https://www.linkedin.com/in/alineleitesap/

https://www.youtube.com/@AlinneLeiteSAP

Como curadora do grupo SAP Community Salvador, meu principal objetivo é promover o compartilhamento de conhecimento e a colaboração entre os membros da comunidade. Estou comprometido em criar um ambiente inclusivo e inspirador, onde todos possam aprender, crescer e contribuir. Minha missão é fortalecer a comunidade, facilitando a discussão, organizando eventos e fornecendo recursos valiosos para apoiar o desenvolvimento profissional e pessoal de cada membro. Juntos, podemos construir uma comunidade vibrante e inovadora.

Vejam como foi o 1º Meet UP Salvador 2025 - https://community.sap.com/t5/salvador-blog-posts/1%C2%BA-meet-up-salvador-de-2025/ba-p/13995980. 

Estou muito animada para conhecer cada um de vocês e ouvir suas histórias. Gostaria de saber onde vocês moram, se trabalham com SAP e, em caso afirmativo, em qual módulo. Além disso, adoraria conhecer seus hobbies e as atividades que você gosta de fazer fora do trabalho. Vamos compartilhar nossas experiências e construir uma comunidade ainda mais forte e conectada!

Este grupo é a nossa casa, onde compartilhamos o espírito de equipe e a paixão pelo aprendizado. Convido cada um de vocês a se unir para criarmos uma comunidade SAP vibrante e solidária em Salvador. Estou aberta para conhecer suas histórias, saber onde vocês moram, se trabalham com SAP e, se sim, em qual módulo. Também quero descobrir seus hobbies e atividades favoritas fora do trabalho. Vamos juntos construir uma rede forte e conectada, onde todos se sintam apoiados e inspirados.

Aguardo com grande entusiasmo seus comentários abaixo!

entry.title: SAP for Me Public Cloud License Consumption Enhanced Data Download
entry.content:
As part of the SAP for Me Finance & Legal dashboard customers can access their Cloud License Consumption in the tab “Consumption”. This helps to monitor how many licenses they are entitled to use and how many licenses have been consumed.

For each licensed metric the tab displays the entitlement, the consumption and the respective delta. In addition, the card offers information on the underlying materials per metric, the underlying contracts, by clicking on the number “Subscribed”, as well as a break-down of the consumption per tenant, by clicking on the number “Measured” (for most metrics).

For a more detailed explanation on the overall Consumption tab, incl. FAQ and a demo video please make sure to visit the License Utilization Information Support portal page.

Now Available: Enhanced Download

The data in the Consumption tab can be viewed within SAP for Me and also downloaded to Excel, in order to run further custom analysis or trend evaluations.

With the recently improved download functionality in the Public Cloud card users are now able to download the above mentioned data, as excel files. This new download will include drop-down menus with information on the materials and pop-up windows with tenant drill-downs.

By clicking the three dots in the top right corner of the card the user can access the Export function.

This export offers three different files to choose from “Current License Consumption”, “Subscription Details” and “Consumption Details”

Depending on your selection you can download up to three separate Excel files:

  • Current License Consumption: This file gives an overview of the primary data displayed in SAP for Me, including a list of the available metrics, the subscribed and measured amount per metric, as well as the respective delta and the utilization percentage.
  • Subscription Details: The subscription details file offers the connection between the consumption metrics and the respective contracts. For each metric the file shows you the underlying material(s), the underlying contract ID(s) (CMS case ID), including information on the contract runtime and the order quantity per contract.
  • Consumption Details: This file offers a breakdown of the consumption per tenant for each metric (where available) as well as the information on the user distribution for S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition FUE metrics CY334 (3SL) and CY48 (2SL).

Further Information

Please use the ‘Feedback’ slider on the right edge of the SAP for Me page of the application to share your feedback with us on the new download as well as the entire Consumption tab.

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the License Utilization Information Support portal.

SAP for Me is made for you!

entry.title: SAP's Pioneering Leap in Artificial Intelligence: Transforming Enterprise Processes with Business AI
entry.content:
SAP has significantly expanded its Artificial Intelligence (AI) footprint, integrating novel Business AI and Generative AI features across its product suite. These innovations are reshaping countless enterprise processes—from core finance operations to user experience—while delivering tangible benefits in time, effort, and cost efficiencies. As an SAP employee and passionate AI advocate, I'm eager to share how AI is now woven into the SAP fabric, the technical bedrock supporting these solutions, and the concrete business processes impacted by AI adoption.

1. The Evolution of SAP's AI Strategy

SAP's AI journey has grown from automating basic tasks to powering data-driven intelligence, predictive insights, and generative content across multiple business domains. In the last half-year, we've seen AI adoption accelerate under two complementary categories:

  1. Business AI: Purpose-built AI applications that address targeted enterprise challenges—like automated financial reconciliations, predictive demand management, or contract intelligence.
  2. Generative AI: Advanced models that generate new text, code, or visuals, fueling innovations in chatbot interactions, code suggestions, and automated content creation. The SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BT) is central to SAP's AI capabilities, standardizing data management, security, and lifecycle services. By providing a cohesive infrastructure, SAP BTP allows organizations to develop, deploy, and govern AI solutions in a scalable, secure manner.

2. Key AI Technologies and Deployment Methodologies

2.1 SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

Embedded in SAP BTP, SAP AI Core provides a robust execution layer for complex machine learning (ML) and deep learning workloads. It connects with SAP AI Launchpad, a central console for data scientists, ML engineers, and developers to orchestrate every stage of the ML lifecycle, including:

  • •Model Development: Create and refine ML models in Jupyter notebooks or other environments.
  • •Model Validation: Assess performance metrics before production.
  • •Deployment: Convert trained models into scalable microservices accessible via APIs.
  • •Monitoring: Track model performance and trigger retraining or decommissioning.

This integrated approach ensures that AI models can be deployed quickly and consistently across diverse SAP applications.

2.2 SAP Data Intelligence

SAP Data Intelligence streamlines large-scale data processing and AI workflows:

  • Data Integration: Connects various data sources (SAP S/4HANA, SAP HANA, external APIs) to unify and prepare data for AI projects.
  • ML Pipelines: This feature enables data scientists to design and run machine learning workflows using containerized microservices, from data preprocessing to model deployment.
  • Scalability: Built on Kubernetes, SAP Data Intelligence scales data pipelines according to workload demands.

Thanks to these features, enterprises can incorporate AI insights into real-time business processes—such as supply chain planning in SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) or forecasting in SAP Analytics Cloud—with minimal complexity.

2.3 SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation (SAP Intelligent RPA)

Repetitive manual tasks can often slow down enterprise operations, which is where SAP Intelligent RPA comes into play:

  • Automation: Configurable bots log into systems, fill fields, and trigger subsequent workflows across SAP and non-SAP environments.
  • AI-Enhanced Capabilities: Document recognition and computer vision allow bots to handle tasks like invoice capture, data validation, and purchase order entry accurately.
  • Central Orchestration: Bots are managed through a cloud-based interface on SAP BTP, ensuring quick deployment and monitoring at scale.

By relieving employees of routine tasks, SAP Intelligent RPA frees them up to tackle more strategic responsibilities.

2.4 SAP Conversational AI

Conversational interfaces dramatically improve user experience, and SAP Conversational AI is SAP's solution for building sophisticated chatbots:

  • Natural Language Processing (NLP): Advanced ML models parse user intent, understand context, and extract data from user conversations.
  • Training Interface: Bot developers can easily import training sentences, label entities, and build language models without deep coding.
  • Omnichannel Deployment: Once created, chatbots can be integrated into websites, social media, and mobile apps and connected to SAP S/4HANA or SAP CX.

The result is a self-service ecosystem where customers and employees get quick, accurate answers, even to complex queries.

3. Embedded AI Throughout SAP's Portfolio

SAP has rapidly extended AI features across its core applications and industry solutions. Notable examples include:

  1. SAP S/4HANA: Incorporates predictive analytics for financial close tasks, anomaly detection in invoices, and ML-based demand planning.
  2. SAP SuccessFactors: Includes AI-driven tools to analyze resumes, match candidates to job roles, and recommend upskilling options.
  3. SAP ISM (e.g. Ariba): Uses intelligent spend analysis, NLP-driven contract analysis, and supplier risk detection.
  4. SAP Fieldglass: Leverages AI for workforce planning, talent pool optimization and compliance checks.
  5. SAP Analytics Cloud: Offers predictive scenario generation and conversational analytics to transform dashboards into proactive insight hubs.
  6. SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP): Embeds AI-based forecasting incorporating internal and external variables—like trends, social sentiment, or climate data—for more accurate planning.

Across all these systems, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and SAP Data Intelligence provide the foundation to ensure seamless AI adoption, robust lifecycle management, and high scalability.

4. Generative AI Use Cases: A Catalyst for Enterprise Innovation

Generative AI is emerging as a powerful tool within the SAP environment, pushing the boundaries of how organizations organize, create and optimize optimal workflows. Recent enhancements revolve around:

  1. Code Generation
    • Generative models help developers by recommending code snippets and configurations tailored to SAP's ABAP environment or other programming languages. This feature speeds development cycles and reduces errors, as the AI model references large repositories of best practices.
  2. Document Summaries
    • Leveraging large language models (LLMs), AI can read lengthy HR policies, contractual documents, or financial statements and produce concise summaries. This helps employees and managers quickly identify key insights without sifting through pages of text.
  3. Content Creation
    • SAP Marketing Cloud users can benefit from AI-driven suggestions for ad copy, mail subject lines, and campaign slogans. Marketers can refine AI-generated text to maintain brand voice, achieving faster marketing time with more engaging content.
  4. Enhanced Chatbots
    • Pairing SAP Conversational AI with generative language models can produce more natural, context-rich responses. This goes beyond simple Q&A bots and delivers a more human-like dialogue experience, improving user satisfaction and employee efficiency.

5. AI-Driven Processes for Enterprise Transformation

Rather than simply citing numbers, seeing how these AI enhancements impact day-to-day enterprise processes is essential. Below are specific workflows illustrating how AI brings tangible benefits in time, effort, and overall operational excellence.

5.1 Financial Invoice Processing

  1. In finance departments, invoice management often involves manual data extraction, validation, and entry into SAP S/4HANA. By combining SAP Intelligent RPA bots with machine learning-based invoice recognition, enterprises can:
    1. Automate Data Extraction: AI reads invoice fields such as supplier name, amount, tax, and dates.
    2. Validate Against Master Data: To flag mismatches, the system compares extracted details to existing vendor records or purchase orders.
    3. Initiate Approvals: Once verified, the invoice automatically moves into the approval workflow in SAP S/4HANA.
  • This end-to-end automation minimizes manual effort, reduces billing errors, and ensures more accurate financial statements.

5.2 Procurement Compliance

  • In procurement, contract management and supplier onboarding can be incredibly time-consuming. SAP Ariba, augmented by NLP and A, I can streamline these steps:
    • Contract Analysis: AI reads contract clauses to identify compliance issues or potential risks, eliminating the need for line-by-line manual review.
    • Supplier Performance Insights: AI synthesizes from multiple touchpoints—like on-time delivery and quality records—to score vendor reliability.
    • Automatic Alerts: If anomalies or mismatches occur between a supplier's invoice and contract terms, the system highlights them for further action.
    • This higher-level visibility ensures procurement teams can focus on negotiating better deals and strengthening supplier relationships rather than being bogged down by mundane checks.

5.3 Customer Support and Self-Service

  • Customer experience is paramount in every industry. AI-infused chatbots in SAP Conversational AI can enhance service processes:
    1. 24/7 Query Handling: Chatbots tackle repetitive requests (e.g., order status, password resets) at any hour.
    2. Tiered Escalation: More complex issues route to human agents, enriched by AI-summarised text so agents can respond faster.
    3. Process Integration: The chatbot triggers workflows in SAP S/4HANA, SAP CX, or other systems for tasks like order cancellations or status updates.
  • Over time, these bots can learn from fundamental interactions, refine answers, and adapt to frequently asked questions to reduce response times.

5.4 Workforce and Talent Management

  • Leveraging AI in HR processes improves talent acquisition, development, and engagement within SAP SuccessFactors:
    1. Resume Screening: Machine learning algorithms match candidate profiles to job requirements, cutting out manual resume filtering.
    2. Skill Gap Detection: The system identifies skills that existing employees need and recommends learning paths in SAP SuccessFactors Learning.
    3. Predictive Retention: AI examines employee turnover patterns, detecting early signs of disengagement or burnout so HR teams can take proactive measures.
  • HR leaders gain a deeper understanding of workforce dynamics through these processes and can quickly implement targeted interventions.

5.5 Supply Chain Forecasting and Planning

  • Supply chains often face volatile market conditions and fluctuating consumer demands. SAP IBP, backed by AI-based forecasting, transforms planning processes:
    1. Data Enrichment: Machine learning models incorporate external signals (e.g., economic indicators and weather forecasts) to improve demand planning.
    2. Inventory Optimization recommends safety stock adjustments based on real-time demand patterns, ensuring products are stocked where and when needed.
    3. Scenario Simulations: Planners run "what-if" simulations to gauge the impact of changing lead times or supplier constraints, all powered by predictive analytics.
  • This approach shortens planning cycles and helps mitigate the risk of overstocking or understocking vital goods.

6. Ensuring Responsible AI Deployment

As AI infiltrates more aspects of enterprise operations, SAP places a strong emphasis on responsible AI:

  1. Data Privacy and Security: Strict governance policies on SAP BTP ensure encryption, anonymization and role-based access.
  2. Ethical Framework: SAP's AI ethics guidelines promote fairness, transparency, and accountability, with regular checks for unintended bias in training data.
  3. Lifecycle Governance: By centralizing the SAP AI Core, companies can monitor model drift, manage versioning, and retrain or retire AI services as needed.

Responsible AI is pivotal in maintaining stakeholder trust and ensuring the long-term viability of AI-driven strategies.

7. What's Next for SAP AI?

We anticipate deeper AI integrations across SAP S/4HANA and other SAP Cloud solutions. Generative AI will extend beyond text and code generation into new domains, such as:

  1. Blueprinting for SAP Solution Manager: Automatically drafting solution documentation.
  2. Image and Video Analysis: AI could identify defects in real-time camera feeds in manufacturing contexts or quality inspections.
  3. Fiori Applications: Embedding multi-modal AI elements—like voice-based commands or augmented reality overlays—within SAP Fiori user interfaces.

SAP's continued collaboration with hyperscalers (e.g., Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google Cloud) and open-source AI communities ensures a steady influx of new capabilities and governance improvements. Simultaneously, expanding low-code/no-code AI development tools will empower more employees to build AI solutions without extensive coding backgrounds.

8. Conclusion

SAP's infusion of AI—spanning Business AI and Generative AI—has rapidly transformed how enterprises can optimize everyday workflows. Through solutions like SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, SAP Data Intelligence, SAP Intelligent RPA, and SAP Conversational AI, organizations have a holistic AI platform that is reliable, scalable, and secure.

By embedding AI into significant products such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP HXM (SuccessFactors), SAP ISM, SAP Fieldglass, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP IBP, SAP has systematically integrated machine learning into finance, procurement, HR, customer support, and supply chain processes. This focus on process transformation—rather than mere numeric gains—demonstrates how AI can accurately handle complex tasks, freeing up human talent for more strategic and creative pursuits.

With each quarter, SAP's AI roadmap continues evolving, delivering enhanced automation, predictive analytics, and intelligent user experiences. As we look to the future, expect even more powerful AI-driven capabilities, deeper business process insights, and broader adoption across industries. This is just the beginning of what AI can achieve within the SAP ecosystem, and enterprises that embrace these innovations now stand to become the Intelligent Enterprises of tomorrow.

#SLIThoughtLeadership

entry.title: Introduction
entry.content: Just wanted to quickly introduce myself on this community. My name is Matt and I have been working on SAP projects for the past year and a bit. I have been upskilling myself in SAP Group Reporting, both practically and via the SAP Learning Journeys. I look forward to engaging and learning from this community, and also hopefully sharing some personal contributions with you all.

entry.title: SAPinsider EMEA 2024 : Au cœur des innovations !
entry.content:
Du 12 au 14 novembre 2024, ce n'est pas à l'Orange Vélodrome de la Cité Phocéenne que notre équipe a eu le plaisir de pouvoir profiter de la tournée de Joule, mais bien à Copenhague, à l'occasion du SAPinsider. 

Ce rassemblement est l’un des plus importants pour les professionnels SAP, étant la nouvelle alternative au SAP TechEd Barcelona et offrant une plateforme idéale pour découvrir les dernières tendances, technologies et meilleures pratiques. Il a attiré un public varié : des développeurs, des consultants fonctionnels, des experts techniques et des administrateurs systèmes. C'était l'occasion parfaite pour échanger, apprendre et s'inspirer des innovations SAP.

Les thématiques clés de l’événement : RISE, Cloud ALM, Joule et Clean Core

L’édition 2024 de SAPinsider s’est concentrée sur des sujets stratégiques qui façonnent l’avenir de l'écosystème SAP. Parmi les thématiques centrales, on retrouve :

  • RISE & GROW with SAP : Un ensemble de solutions permettant aux entreprises d'accélérer leur transformation digitale avec SAP S/4HANA Cloud et des outils associés.
  • Cloud ALM : Une approche clé pour gérer le cycle de vie des applications SAP dans le cloud, optimisant ainsi la performance et la fiabilité tout au long du parcours.
  • Clean Core : Une stratégie visant à simplifier et rationaliser les systèmes SAP en réduisant les personnalisations excessives, afin de garantir une base plus évolutive et facile à maintenir.
  • Joule & SAP Build : L'intégration de l'IA et de l'automatisation dans les processus SAP, avec un focus sur Joule 4 ABAP (version bêta), permettant de générer des applications Fiori sans compétences techniques avancées.

Ces thématiques sont non seulement des tendances fortes, mais aussi des solutions interconnectées qui aident les entreprises à mieux se préparer aux défis de la transformation numérique.

Des sessions pratiques et des retours d'expérience clients

L'événement s'est distingué par des sessions interactives où des experts SAP ont partagé les dernières évolutions et stratégies pour adopter ces technologies. Les participants ont eu l'opportunité de découvrir des solutions concrètes grâce aux retours d'expérience clients (REX), illustrant les réussites et les défis des entreprises dans l'implémentation des technologies évoquées.

Une mention spéciale a été faite pour l’automatisation des tests avec Tosca, un outil qui permet de simplifier et accélérer les tests dans les environnements SAP complexes. Grâce à son approche sans script, Tosca facilite la création de tests automatisés, réduisant ainsi les coûts de gestion manuelle tout en garantissant des tests complets et une intégration fluide dans les processus DevOps.

Ateliers pratiques et Developer Garage

Les ateliers pratiques ont été un moment fort de l’événement. Les Developer Advocates comme @qmacro (DJ Adams) , @KRiedelsheimer ,   @Rekha_DR  et @ajmaradiaga ont animé des sessions hands-on, plongeant les participants dans des technologies innovantes telles que CAP, RAP et Joule. Une attention particulière a été portée sur Joule 4 ABAP, présenté en avant-première dans sa version bêta, ce qui a suscité un grand intérêt chez les développeurs.

Le Developer Garage a offert une expérience interactive où les participants ont pu relever des défis et explorer des sujets comme les CDS Views, RAP et CAP à travers des tutoriels conçus pour tous les niveaux. Cette expérience a combiné apprentissage et plaisir, avec des solutions concrètes à découvrir à chaque étape.

Networking et partage d’expériences

Au-delà des conférences et ateliers, SAPinsider a été un lieu de rencontre et de réseautage pour la communauté SAP. Les participants ont eu l'opportunité de tisser des liens avec des professionnels du monde entier, d’échanger sur les défis de la transformation numérique et de découvrir des perspectives nouvelles sur l’utilisation de SAP. Ce fut également un excellent moyen de s’inspirer des meilleures pratiques et de repartir avec de nouvelles idées pour améliorer les projets et les compétences des équipes.

Cet événement était une belle occasion d’apprendre, de réseauter et de découvrir des solutions concrètes pour tirer parti des innovations SAP. Nous sommes repartis avec plein d’idées pour améliorer nos projets et nos compétences. A refaire l'année prochaine 😉

entry.title: 10.7.2025 - HANA Tech Con
entry.content:
𝐆𝐞𝐭 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐞𝐝! …and mark your calendars for July 10th. We are proud to announce the first-ever happening of HANA Tech Con – a community-driven tech conference on-site in St. Leon-Rot, Germany!

🤝HANA Tech Con is organized by the community around SAP HANA. This new conference brings together developers, users, and partners to exchange and share their knowledge!

🫵 There are many way to become part of this vibrant community of SAP HANA experts! Consider contributing content, sponsoring, or simply attending the conference. More details here: https://hanatech.community

🙌Our conference is part of the Code Connect 2025 – one week, three events! Attend UI5Con, re>≡CAP, and HANA Tech Con in one run: https://code-connect.dev/

entry.title: Comunidade SIT Brazil
entry.content:
Este blog tratrá atualizações do que está ocorrendo pela Comunidade SAP no Brasil e seus grupos locais!

https://sitbrazil.com/

entry.title: Página Web da Comunidade SAP do Brasil está no ar pela 1ª vez!
entry.content:
Agora é possível ver tudo que está acontecendo na Comunidade SAP no Brasil em 1 só lugar com um visual desenhado em alto nível.

Visitem: https://sitbrazil.com/

e não esqueçam de dizer o que acharam!

entry.title: A comunidade SAP no Brasil tem seu primeiro site no ar
entry.content:
A comunidade SAP no Brasil acaba de estrear seu primeiro site. Agora, você poderá saber todas as novidadades da comunidade em um só lugar. 

O site está incrível! Visitem: https://sitbrazil.com/  e não esqueçam de dizer o que acharam.
